audit.title: Audit log
audit.subtitle: An immutable record of every sensitive action — who did what and when (cannot be edited or deleted).
audit.adminSubtitle: Cross-account audit log — with actor names and IP addresses (for regulator review).
audit.immutableNote: 🔒 This log is immutable: edits/deletes are rejected even from code — an NCA ECC requirement.

firmOrder.title: Confirm firm order
firmOrder.desc: “{insurer}” will be selected as the final quotation and the request moves to issuance.

renewal.title: Start renewal
renewal.desc: A renewal request will be created for this policy, starting a new cycle.

approveClient.title: Approve client
approveClient.desc: The client will be compliance-approved and policies can be issued.

rejectClient.title: Reject client
rejectClient.desc: The client will be rejected and requests cannot proceed (reversible later).

startRfq.title: Start RFQ
startRfq.desc: A slip will be created to collect insurer quotations and the request moves to quoting.

issuePolicy.title: Issue policy
issuePolicy.desc: A policy will be issued from this awarded request and moved to production.

approveTechnical.title: Technical approval
approveTechnical.desc: The policy will be technically approved and move to financial approval.

approveFinance.title: Financial approval
approveFinance.desc: Financial approval auto-generates a journal voucher, debit note and tax invoice, and the policy becomes active.

approveStep.title: Approve step
approveStep.desc: You will approve the configured extra approval step for this policy (per the approval chain).

revert.title: Revert one step
revert.desc: The policy will be moved one step back to its previous state (the action is audited). Issued policies cannot be reverted directly.

claimStatus.title: Update claim status
claimStatus.desc: The claim status will change to “{status}”.

serviceStatus.title: Update request status
serviceStatus.desc: The service request status will change to “{status}”.

tenantSuspend.title: Suspend account
tenantSuspend.desc: Account “{name}” access to the platform will be suspended immediately.

tenantActivate.title: Activate account
tenantActivate.desc: Account “{name}” access to the platform will be restored.

runYaqeen.title: Confirm verification
runYaqeen.desc: A Yaqeen identity check will run and one paid operation will be deducted from your wallet.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Your priorities today — sorted by urgency
dashboard.renewalsDist: Policies due for renewal within 60 days

reports.title: Reports & analytics
reports.subtitle: Production, commissions, claims and regulator reports — live data

scheduled.title: Scheduled reports
scheduled.subtitle: Email a periodic report summary to company management
scheduled.empty: No scheduled reports — add one above

finance.title: Finance & accounting
finance.subtitle: Chart of accounts, ZATCA tax invoices, and fiduciary separation
finance.receivablesSub: Debit notes owed by clients and installment plans
finance.invoicesSub: Correct direction: commission invoiced to the insurer, service fees invoiced to the client — each with a compliant QR (TLV)

payroll.title: Payroll
payroll.subtitle: Monthly salary runs; posting creates an expense voucher (Dr Salaries / Cr Cash)
payroll.selectRun: Select a run to view its lines

budget.title: Budget vs Actual
budget.subtitle: Set each account's budget for the year/quarter and compare against actuals derived from vouchers
budget.empty: No budget lines for this period — add an account above

finance.payablesSub: Net premium held per insurer — with aging and amounts settled
finance.trialBalanceSub: Voucher entries aggregated by account — debit must equal credit

overview.incomeStatementSub: Brokerage income on accrual basis (earned at policy issuance)
overview.vatNote: Note: VAT is not revenue — it is collected on behalf of ZATCA and remitted to it, so it is excluded from the income statement.
overview.trustSub: Collected premiums to remit to insurers

journal.title: Journal entry / expense
journal.sub: Record a balanced accounting entry or an operating expense

commissions.employeesSub: Sales-staff incentive — earned when brokerage commission is collected
commissions.accruedHint: Total commission on their issued policies (before collection)
commissions.eligibleHint: Actually earned (brokerage commission collected from insurer)
commissions.emptyEmp: No employee commissions — set an employee rate on their page then issue policies
commissions.note: Earned on collection: commission is expected at issuance and becomes earned when the brokerage commission is collected from the insurer. Payment posts an expense to Employee commissions & incentives.
commissions.producersSub: Producers share of the brokerage commission

receivablesTab.empty: No receivables — debit notes issued to clients appear here

installments.none: No installment plan for this note
installments.note: Installments split the note total into payments — either equal monthly, or a custom schedule with a date and amount per installment. Receipts are applied automatically to the oldest-due installments first, and the client is reminded before and on the due date.
installments.firstDueHint: Leave empty to start today — installments recur monthly
installments.customHint: Set the date and amount of each installment

instTrack.title: Installment tracking
instTrack.sub: Due dates and overdue across all clients

balanceSheet.title: Balance sheet
balanceSheet.sub: Statement of financial position — derived from the trial balance
balanceSheet.offBalanceNote: This statement includes client trust funds (held for the insurer) of SAR {amount} — a fiduciary off-balance-sheet item.
balanceSheet.unclassified: Note: {count} account(s) without a type classification — please review the chart of accounts.

ledger.title: General ledger
ledger.hint: Click any account to open its general ledger (movement with running balance).

vat.title: VAT return
vat.sub: Output − input = net due to the authority (ZATCA) — leave the period empty for all-time
vat.note: The return is derived from the two VAT ledger accounts (output 0203 · input 0105). Zero-rated sales (life insurance) carry no output VAT. The standard taxable base is estimated from the tax (÷15%).

cashflow.title: Cash flow statement
cashflow.sub: Direct method — cash & bank movements classified by activity · leave the period empty for all-time
cashflow.operatingSub: Client collections · insurer settlements · expenses, salaries & commissions · VAT
cashflow.none: No movement in this activity for the period
cashflow.note: The statement is derived by the direct method from cash & bank account (0101) movements in their vouchers, classifying each movement by its contra account. Net change = operating + investing + financing = closing − opening (deterministic reconciliation by double entry).

producers.title: Sub-brokers
producers.subtitle: Registry of those who bring business for a share of the brokerage commission — with their owed and settled commission ledger
producers.empty: No sub-brokers yet — add the first one

premiums.title: Premiums & collections
premiums.subtitle: Amounts due from clients via debit notes
premiums.agingSub: Outstanding per client by overdue period — for collection priorities

compliance.title: Compliance & risk
compliance.subtitle: Client compliance status and PEP/sanctions risk distribution

integrations.title: Regulatory integrations
integrations.subtitle: Government connectors and their status — Sandbox in development

clients.title: Clients
clients.subtitle: Manage both corporate and individual clients
clients.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, CR, ID or mobile…

verify.title: Confirm government verification
verify.desc: A “{provider}” check will run on the client's data, one operation will be deducted from the wallet, and the result linked to the client file.
verify.done: “{name}” verified via {provider} and linked to the file.
verify.missingId: Cannot verify: this client has no CR / national ID.

clients.name: Client name

commissions.title: Commissions
commissions.subtitle: Track accrued, received and variance commissions

placeholder.title: Under construction
placeholder.body: This screen will be built in a later ROADMAP phase. The UI here is scaffolding only.

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Insurance Brokerage Management System
login.mfaHint: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

tenantCompany.title: Company details
tenantCompany.subtitle: Your brokerage information — appears on invoices and official correspondence.
tenantCompany.name: Company name (Arabic)
tenantCompany.orgDesc: Your departments, default roles and hierarchy chart.
tenantCompany.addressHint: Appears on the tax invoice (ZATCA requirement).

tenantPayment.title: Payment gateway
tenantPayment.subtitle: Collect payments from your clients (installments and balances) via a payment gateway using your own keys.
tenantPayment.gatewayHint: Choose one gateway to enable for collecting payments. “None” disables online payment.
tenantPayment.secretHint: The secret key is stored encrypted at-rest and never shown again. Leave empty to keep the current one.
tenantPayment.badPublic: Public key must start with pk_test_ (test) or pk_live_ (live)
tenantPayment.badSecret: Secret key must start with sk_test_ (test) or sk_live_ (live)
tenantPayment.modeMismatch: Public and secret keys are different modes — both must be test or both live

mode.testHint: Test keys — no real charges; for trials before going live
mode.liveHint: Live keys — real charges will be made to clients’ cards
mode.hint: Enter test keys to try, or live keys for production — mode is auto-detected from the key prefix

tenantPayment.enableHint: requires a gateway and a secret key
tenantPayment.securityNote: 🔒 The secret key is encrypted with AES-256-GCM and never exposed in the UI or logs. Your keys are private to your company and fully isolated from other tenants.

adminPayment.title: Platform payment gateway (Tap)
adminPayment.subtitle: Tap keys for collecting broker subscriptions through the platform — with a test↔production toggle.
adminPayment.activeModeHint: Determines which keys are actually used to collect subscriptions. Switching needs no re-entry — both modes are stored.
adminPayment.merchantIdHint: Your Tap merchant account ID — for reference and reconciliation.
adminPayment.secretHint: Stored encrypted at-rest and never shown again. Leave empty to keep the current one.
adminPayment.badTestPublic: Test public key must start with pk_test_
adminPayment.badTestSecret: Test secret key must start with sk_test_
adminPayment.badLivePublic: Live public key must start with pk_live_
adminPayment.badLiveSecret: Live secret key must start with sk_live_
adminPayment.enableNeedsKeys: Cannot enable: complete both “{mode}” keys first
adminPayment.securityNote: Secret keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM and never exposed in the UI or logs. Managed exclusively from this panel and never stored in the repository.

tenantEmail.title: Official email & notifications
tenantEmail.subtitle: Send your company notifications from your official domain via your own Resend account — auto-linked with DNS verification.
tenantEmail.apiKeyKeep: Leave blank to keep the current key
tenantEmail.apiKeyHint: Optional. Without it: we send via the central platform domain and replies reach your inbox. With it: we send from your own domain (BYO). Encrypted and never displayed — create it from Resend → API Keys.
tenantEmail.fallbackBanner: Currently sending via the central domain temporarily ({from}) — it will switch to your email once verification completes.
tenantEmail.replyOnlyBanner: Sending via the platform domain ({from}); recipients' replies reach your official inbox ({reply}). Add a Resend key to send from your own domain.
tenantEmail.dnsSubtitle: Add these records at your {domain} DNS provider, then click “Verify now”.

tenantBranding.title: Branding
tenantBranding.subtitle: Define your company logo and color — reflected across the app, client portal, documents and email.
tenantBranding.logoHint: PNG/JPG/SVG/WebP — up to 512KB. Served via a stable public URL (shows in email).
tenantBranding.previewNote: This is how your company colors will appear across the platform and your client portal.

ownership.heroTitle: License your operating platform — an enterprise alternative
ownership.heroSub: An enterprise alternative to recurring subscription — a perpetual license with source code and a dedicated environment, for brokerage groups and large entities (NX Solutions retains the intellectual property).
ownership.tier1Tagline: Perpetual use license with full source access.
ownership.tier1P1: Perpetual use license with no recurring subscription fees
ownership.tier1P3: Freedom to deploy and modify internally
ownership.tier1P4: Single restriction: no resale or redistribution
ownership.tier2Tagline: Perpetual license with source code and a dedicated isolated environment — NX Solutions retains the intellectual property.
ownership.tier2P1: Extended perpetual use license with no recurring fees
ownership.tier2P2: Full source code + a dedicated isolated environment
ownership.tier2P3: Full handover and knowledge transfer + deeper customization
ownership.tier2P4: NX Solutions retains the IP (no resale or redistribution)
ownership.deploySub: On your own servers (on-premise) or an isolated private cloud — per your company policy and data sovereignty.
ownership.supportSub: An optional annual package — activate it whenever you wish; your ownership never depends on it.
ownership.supportP2: New government connectors as they become available
ownership.pillar1Desc: An enterprise alternative to recurring subscription — accounting treatment (capitalization/amortization) depends on the contract nature and your organization's policies.
ownership.pillar2Desc: No recurring subscriptions — one cost and perpetual ownership.
ownership.pillar3Desc: Complete control over data, compliance and in-Kingdom hosting.
ownership.pillar4Desc: Shape the system to your brand and needs without vendor limits.
ownership.contactSub: Tell us about your organization and requirements, and we'll come back with a tailored proposal.

invoiceDoc.title: Tax Invoice
invoiceDoc.footer: Issued electronically via {brand} — no signature required.

coverNoteDoc.title: Temporary cover note
coverNoteDoc.disclaimer: This is a temporary cover note confirming that cover is in force on the terms above until the final insurance policy is issued or the note expires, whichever is earlier. The final policy supersedes it upon issuance.
coverNoteDoc.footer: Issued electronically via {brand} — no signature required.

voucherDoc.title: Voucher
voucherDoc.footer: Voucher issued electronically via {brand} — no signature required.

bank.title: Bank reconciliation
bank.subtitle: Bank accounts + import the bank statement and match it to system vouchers to surface differences
bank.noAccounts: No bank accounts yet — add one to start.
bank.selectAccount: Select an account to view its reconciliation.
bank.noTxns: No transactions — import the bank statement.
bank.importHint: Paste statement lines — each line: date,description,amount,reference (positive = deposit · negative = withdrawal).

insurers.title: Insurers
insurers.subtitle: Insurer registry & agreements — commission rates, settlement cycle, bank account — with each insurer's production.
insurers.empty: No insurers yet — add the first one
insurers.name: Name

targets.title: Targets & performance
targets.subtitle: Set production targets for sub-brokers and insurance lines, and measure achievement from actual production.
targets.empty: No targets yet — add the first one
targets.note: Actuals are computed from policies issued within the period — no new data.

tenantSecurity.title: Security & 2FA
tenantSecurity.subtitle: Add an extra layer of protection to your account via an authenticator app (TOTP).
tenantSecurity.scan: Scan the code in your authenticator app (Google Authenticator/Authy) or enter the secret manually, then confirm with a code:
tenantSecurity.requiredNote: Your company mandates 2FA — it cannot be disabled.
tenantSecurity.enrollNow: Your company requires two-factor authentication. Enable it now to secure your account.
tenantSecurity.orgSubtitle: Require all staff to enable two-factor authentication.
tenantSecurity.retentionSubtitle: How long client data is retained before destruction (PDPL/Insurance Authority). Default 10 years.

signup.title: Create your company account
signup.subtitle: Get your platform ready in minutes — pay after the trial
signup.emailTaken: This email is already in use — use another or sign in
signup.passwordHint: At least 8 chars with an uppercase, lowercase and a digit
signup.perUserNote: add any number — priced per user

grow.title: Pay as you grow
grow.sub: Start small and scale when you need to — never pay for seats you don't use.
grow.fair: Transparent breakdown: per-user price × number of users — no hidden fees.

signup.noCard: No credit card to start — cancel anytime

contact.title: Talk to sales
contact.sub: Leave your details and our team will reach out to tailor an enterprise plan and quote for your size.
contact.name: Name
contact.thanksSub: Our sales team will contact you shortly.

compare.title: Compare plans
compare.heading: Compare plans
compare.sub: Every plan includes the operational and IA-compliance essentials. Tiers differ on advanced features and quotas.
compare.footnote: Any firm can upgrade anytime. Prices are per user.

billing.title: Subscription & Billing
billing.subtitle: Manage your plan and payments
billing.trialBanner: Your free trial ends on {date} — {days} days left. Payment will be due after that to activate the plan.
billing.seatsModel: Pre-paid model: active users are capped by your licensed seats. During the trial = the seats chosen at signup; to add more, buy extra seats (raise the license) and your membership updates with your seat count.
billing.buySeatsHint: Price per extra seat, prorated for the remaining {cycle} period ≈ {amount} {currency} ({days} days). The license is raised once payment completes.
billing.buySeatsHintFull: Price per extra seat = the per-user price for a full {cycle} cycle ≈ {amount} {currency}. The license is raised once payment completes.
billing.addUnitHint: Adding a user now ≈ {amount} {currency} for the remaining {days} days.
billing.pendingCredit: Prorated credit: {amount} {currency} applied to renewal (users removed).
billing.licenseTransfer: Transfer: when an employee leaves, deactivate their account and activate a replacement — the count stays the same, at no extra charge.
billing.licenseCancel: Cancel: deactivating an employee without a replacement lowers the user count, so the next renewal is automatically reduced.

return.success: Payment received — your subscription is active

org.title: Org structure & departments
org.subtitle: Organize departments hierarchically and set each one's default role
org.chartHint: View the company as an org chart — each department with its member count and default role.
org.empty: No departments yet — start by adding a top-level one
org.deleteConfirm: Delete this department? Its members and sub-departments will be detached.

notif.title: Notifications & messages
notif.subtitlePlatform: Default texts and channels for all accounts (companies can customize)
notif.subtitleTenant: Customize the notification texts and channels sent to your clients
notif.subject: Subject
notif.audienceClientHint: Sent to the company's clients via email/SMS
notif.audienceStaffHint: Sent to relevant staff + the account owner (email)
notif.subtitleTenantAll: Customize the texts and channels of all notifications (clients & staff)
notif.inAppNote: In-app notifications (the bell) are always on for every event and cannot be turned off. The options below enable/disable email and SMS per event.

roleMatrix.title: Staff notification routing by role
roleMatrix.subtitle: Choose which roles receive each staff notification type. Default: every role with the module's permission receives it.
roleMatrix.hint: ✔ = receives. Unchecking mutes the type for that role (does not remove its permission). Direct-assignment notifications (task/deal assigned to you) always arrive.
roleMatrix.channels: These notifications appear in-app (the bell at the top), and are also delivered by email and/or SMS depending on each event's setup in Notification Settings.

leave.title: Leave requests
leave.subtitle: Submit and approve leave requests (lightweight HR layer)

crm.title: CRM
crm.subtitle: Deals pipeline + tasks & reminders + activity log
crm.convertHint: An insurance request is created from the lead data and enters the underwriting cycle; the lead is marked Won.
crm.activityPlaceholder: Log a note or the outcome of a call/meeting…

cancel.title: Cancel policy & return premium
cancel.hint: Return premium is computed pro-rata on the unexpired days, and a credit note is issued to the client.
cancel.done: Policy cancelled — credit note {seq}, return premium {amount}

endo.title: Add policy endorsement
endo.onlyIssued: Endorsements can only be added to issued policies
endo.cancellationHint: For a full cancellation use the “Cancel policy” button to auto-calculate the pro-rata refund. This endorsement is for partial changes.
endo.willDebit: A debit note (additional premium) will be issued to the client
endo.willCredit: A credit note (premium refund) will be issued to the client

complaints.title: Complaints register
complaints.subtitle: Log and handle client complaints within the resolution SLA and escalate to the Insurance Authority — with a regulatory report.

aml.title: Anti-Money Laundering
aml.subtitle: Client risk rating · sanctions & PEP screening · Suspicious Transaction Reports (STR) — licensing compliance
aml.factorsHint: Select the applicable risk factors — the score and level are computed automatically (a sanctions hit forces high):
aml.fileNow: File immediately (instead of saving as draft)

erase.title: Erase client personal data
erase.desc: All personal data (name/national ID/contact/IBAN) will be permanently masked to fulfil the right to erasure (PDPL), while financial records are retained for audit. This cannot be undone.

portal.inviteTitle: Invite the client to their portal
portal.inviteSub: Creates an account and sends an activation link for them to set their own password
portal.sent: Invite created — the link was emailed; you can also copy and share it manually:
portal.revokeDesc: “{email}” will be blocked from the client portal (you can re-invite later).
portal.empty: No portal users yet — invite the client to log in and follow their policies and claims

mfa.label: 2FA
mfa.resetDesc: Two-factor authentication will be disabled for this employee (e.g. lost device); they can re-enroll themselves later. Confirm?

staffDetail.permsNote: This employee's permissions derive from their role (RBAC). “Access” = whether the module appears in their menu; the other columns = create/edit/delete/revert rights. Edit at the role level (applies to all its members) via “Roles & Permissions”.

scope.title: Product scope (line-level access)
scope.hint: Restricts this employee’s view & creation of requests/policies to specific insurance lines. None = all lines.

commission.title: Sales commission/incentive
commission.hint: Employee share of the brokerage commission on policies they issue (leave empty for none)

approvalChain.title: Policy approval chain
approvalChain.subtitle: Extra approval steps between technical and finance approval — set by the account owner
approvalChain.defaultNote: No extra steps — the policy flows directly: technical approval ⇐ finance approval.
approvalChain.technicalGateHint: A technical review layer before finance approval — can be disabled
approvalChain.segregationHint: The policy issuer cannot approve it financially — regulatory recommendation (Insurance Authority / NCA)
approvalChain.technicalSegHint: The policy issuer cannot approve its own technical review or extra steps — enforces maker-checker across the whole pre-financial chain (optional)
approvalChain.freeLookHint: Days during which the client may cancel the policy for a full refund (0 = disabled; set it per the Insurance Authority's guidance for your products)

staff.title: Staff
staff.subtitle: Manage employees and their permission matrix
staff.seatFull: You've reached your licensed seats ({limit}). Buy more seats to add new employees.
staff.name: Name
staff.forbidden: You don't have staff management permission (settings).

roles.title: Roles & Permissions
roles.subtitle: Create roles and edit their permission matrix per module — fine-grained control over each employee's access.
roles.name: Role name
roles.deleteConfirm: Delete role "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
roles.inUse: Role is assigned to users — cannot delete
roles.pickHint: Select a role to edit its permissions, or create a new one.

requests.title: Requests
requests.subtitle: Insurance requests (RFQ) for clients
requests.empty: No requests yet — start a new request
requests.noMatch: No requests match the current filters

underwritingQueue.title: Underwriting
underwritingQueue.subtitle: Quotation (RFQ) queue — all slips in underwriting and their quote tracking
underwritingQueue.empty: No slips yet — start from Requests via the “Prepare quotes” button

policies.title: Policies
policies.subtitle: Issuance & approval cascade (technical → finance)
policies.empty: No policies yet — issue from an awarded request

verification.title: Government Verification
verification.subtitle: KYC/KYB via Yaqeen/Wathiq/Nafath/National Address + PEP/sanctions screening
verification.smartFill: Sandbox pull to smart-fill the form (Yaqeen)

service.title: Customer Service
service.subtitle: Client requests (add/delete/amend) and follow-up
service.subject: Subject
service.notePlaceholder: Add a note or update to this request…

detail.name: Name
detail.internalPlaceholder: Internal note — not visible to the client…
detail.replyPlaceholder: Reply shown to the client in their portal…

claims.title: Claims
claims.subtitle: Claim lifecycle: received → submitted → settled → closed
claims.locked: Claims module is not enabled in your plan

renewals.title: Renewals
renewals.subtitle: Renewals board — expired and upcoming policies, ranked by urgency
renewals.empty: No policies due for renewal in this window
renewals.initiated: Renewal request {seq} created — track it under “Requests”
renewals.confirmDesc: A new insurance request will be created from this policy's data and enter the quoting/issuance cycle. No reminder is sent to the client automatically.

underwriting.title: Underwriting
underwriting.insurerPickHint: Pick a registered insurer or type a name

rfq.title: Send RFQ to insurers
rfq.hint: A quotation request is emailed to the selected insurers from your company domain; their replies come straight back to you. For insurers with no email on file, type one to send.
rfq.noInsurers: No insurers registered — add underwriting emails on the Insurers page first

present.title: Present proposals to client
present.desc: The current quotations ({count}) will be shown to the client in their portal to accept or decline. Your commission details are hidden from them.

coverNote.hint: Firm order is in place — you can issue a temporary cover note confirming cover until the policy is issued.

underwriting.noQuotations: No quotations yet — add insurer offers

hint.insurer: Name of the insurer submitting this quotation
hint.sumInsured: Insured value (basis for premium & commission) — individuals or corporates
hint.rate: Premium rate as a % of the sum insured — drives the net premium
hint.premium: Annual net premium before fees and VAT
hint.policyFees: Insurer policy issuance fees (separate from premium)
hint.vat: Value-added tax (15%) on the net premium
hint.totalPremium: Total due from the client = net premium + policy fees + VAT
hint.commissionRate: Brokerage commission rate on the net premium (broker revenue)
hint.commissionAmount: Brokerage commission value = net premium × commission rate
hint.commissionVat: VAT (15%) on the brokerage commission — broker output VAT, always taxable even for life policies
hint.deductible: Amount the client bears on each claim before cover applies
hint.limit: Maximum insurer liability (the policy's cover ceiling)
hint.remarks: Free-text conditions, exclusions and add-ons — shown in the comparison sheet

underwriting.autoNote: VAT, totals and commission are computed automatically from the inputs

requestForm.title: New insurance request
requestForm.subtitle: Pick a client and product to generate the right form for the insurance type
requestForm.saveTemplateHint: Save the current entries as a reusable template for this product — speeds up recurring requests.
requestForm.selectProduct: Select a product to render its dynamic form
requestForm.noClientsYet: No clients registered yet. Add a client to create a request.
requestForm.clientAdded: Client added. It may need compliance approval before completing the request.

leads.title: Sales contact requests
leads.subtitle: “Contact us” requests from large plans and the ownership page — for follow-up.
leads.name: Name

mfa.title: Two-factor authentication (2FA)
mfa.subtitle: Extra protection for the platform account (security requirement)
mfa.scan: Scan the code in your authenticator app (Google Authenticator/Authy), or enter the secret manually:
mfa.loginHint: Enter the code from your authenticator app

tenants.title: Accounts
tenants.subtitle: Brokerage company accounts subscribed to the platform and their subscription status

plans.title: Plans & entitlements
plans.subtitle: Control the modules and limits available per plan
plans.legendHint: click any feature to cycle through these four

portal.subtitle: Track your policies, claims and account

fileClaim.title: File a new claim
fileClaim.done: We received your claim {seq} and will follow up

fileService.title: New service request
fileService.subject: Subject
fileService.done: We received your request {seq} and will follow up

renew.window: Policy valid until {date} — renewal opens {days} days before expiry.

installments.title: Installments
installments.subtitle: Your installments across all policies — paid, due, and overdue
installments.note: Your payments are applied automatically to the oldest-due installments first, and you are reminded before and on each due date.

proposals.title: Insurance proposals
proposals.subtitle: Review the proposals your broker prepared and accept the one that suits you — your policy is issued as soon as you accept
proposals.empty: No proposals presented to you yet.
proposals.pickHint: Choose the option that suits you best
proposals.acceptedNote: You accepted this proposal — your broker is now issuing your policy, and the payment notice will appear in your statement.
proposals.declinedNote: You declined these proposals — your broker will reach out with alternatives.

activate.title: Activate your account
activate.subtitle: Set your password to access the client portal
activate.weak: Password must be at least 8 characters
activate.invalidLink: The invite link is invalid or expired — contact your broker for a new invite.

dashboard.title: Home
dashboard.subtitle: A quick look at your insurance, claims and account

statement.title: Statement of account
statement.subtitle: Debit notes, tax invoices and outstanding balance

pay.confirmingSub: We’re checking your transaction status with the payment gateway.

resultSub.paid: We received your payment and a receipt was recorded automatically in your account.
resultSub.pending: The transaction is not confirmed yet — it may take a moment. Check your statement.
resultSub.failed: The payment did not complete. You can try again from your statement.

pay.secured: Secure payment via a certified gateway

documents.title: My documents
documents.subtitle: Your policies and documents — opened via a secure temporary link

serviceDetail.noMessages: No messages yet — you can write a reply below.

account.title: My account
account.subtitle: Your details and contact information
account.name: Name
account.verifiedHint: These details are verified with your broker and cannot be edited here — contact them for any correction.

hero.title: The Operating System for Insurance Brokerage
hero.titleEn: InsurTech SaaS Platform for Insurance Brokers
hero.subtitle: A multi-tenant SaaS platform that automates the entire insurance-brokerage workflow — from client and underwriting to policy issuance, finance, claims and reporting — with granular permissions and designed to support Saudi compliance requirements.

features.title: Everything a brokerage needs, in one platform
features.subtitle: Integrated modules covering the workflow end to end.

lifecycle.title: Full deal lifecycle
lifecycle.desc: Sales → pricing → production → service → claims → finance, with governed state machines and gates.

underwriting.title: Underwriting & RFQ
underwriting.desc: Request quotes, auto comparison sheet that picks the best offer, firm order in one click.

finance.title: Finance & e-invoicing
finance.desc: Balanced double-entry, 17-digit chart of accounts, and invoices built to the ZATCA Phase 2 spec.

claims.title: Claims & client service
claims.desc: Full claims cycle, service requests and renewals with status tracking.

verification.title: Government verification
verification.desc: Ready-to-activate connectors: Yaqeen · Wathiq · Nafath · National Address · PEP screening — smart-fill (activated with the provider contract).

multitenant.title: Multi-tenant & analytics
multitenant.desc: Strict per-firm data isolation, granular RBAC, and live analytics dashboards.

target.title: Built specifically for insurance brokers
target.desc: Replace scattered systems and spreadsheets with one operating system that runs every operation and keeps you regulator-ready.
target.p1: An independent portal per brokerage (tenant) with its own data, settings and plan.
target.p2: A client portal to track policies, claims, statements and documents.
target.p3: A super-admin console to manage plans, subscriptions and usage.
target.p4: Ready-to-activate government connectors (ZATCA · Nafath · Yaqeen · Wathiq) + reports designed for the insurance sector.

pricing.title: Plans that grow with your firm
pricing.subtitle: Per-user pricing — subscribe monthly or yearly, enable only what you need.
pricing.ownershipIntro: Instead of a monthly subscription, your enterprise owns the full platform — a perpetual license or full source ownership, with a dedicated environment and optional support.
pricing.anyUsers: Unlimited users — pay only for active users

basic.name: Basic
basic.tagline: The full brokerage operations cycle for startups and small firms
basic.f3: Core finance, verification and basic reports

premium.name: Professional
premium.tagline: The natural plan for most brokerages
premium.f2: ZATCA, advanced finance, approval chains and quote comparison
premium.f3: CRM, producers, commissions, analytics, org structure + mandatory MFA

enterprise.name: Enterprise
enterprise.f3: HR, advanced audit log, priority support and SLA

compliance.title: Designed to support Saudi compliance requirements
compliance.ia: Reports designed for the insurance sector
compliance.nafath: Connectors: Nafath · Yaqeen · Wathiq (ready to activate)

cta.title: Ready to run your brokerage professionally?
cta.subtitle: Start today and move your operations onto one operating system.

legal.contactBody: For any question about these documents or data processing, contact the NX Solutions team via the contact form on the home page.
legal.disclaimer: These documents form a general contractual framework for the NX-IBP platform and may be supplemented by a signed subscription agreement; in case of conflict, the signed agreement prevails.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of the NX-IBP insurance-brokerage operations platform, and are entered into between the brokerage (the Customer) and NX Solutions (the Provider).

sections.b: “Platform” means the NX-IBP system and its cloud services. “Customer” is the subscribing brokerage. “User” is any employee account created under the Customer’s subscription. “Customer Data” is everything entered into the Platform by the Customer, its users, or its clients.

Self-service sign-up is available for standard plans with a stated trial period; the Enterprise plan is offered via the sales team. The Customer confirms the accuracy of its entity details (CR, unified number, VAT number) and is responsible for the confidentiality of login credentials.

Pricing is based on the number of actual active users with no plan seat cap; users added mid-cycle are prorated for the remaining period. VAT is added per applicable regulations, and fees are payable in advance each cycle.

The Customer will use the Platform for its lawful insurance-brokerage purposes in compliance with the laws of the Kingdom, and will not attempt to breach it, resell it, misuse its resources, or upload unlawful content.

The Customer owns its client and business data entered into the Platform. Personal data is processed per the attached Privacy Policy and Data Processing Agreement, consistent with the principles of the Personal Data Protection Law.

All intellectual property rights in the Platform, its code, and its marks remain owned by NX Solutions. The subscription grants only a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use, and transfers no intellectual property.

The Platform is provided “as is” with reasonable professional care. The Provider is not liable for indirect or consequential damages, and its aggregate liability — to the extent permitted by law — is limited to the fees paid in the twelve months preceding the event.
Either party may terminate per the agreed term. On termination the Customer may export its data within a reasonable period, after which data is deleted or returned per the Data Processing Agreement.
These terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and disputes are settled before the competent judicial authorities in the Kingdom.
The Provider may update these terms with reasonable prior notice via the Platform or email. Continued use after an update takes effect constitutes acceptance.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains how NX-IBP handles personal data, and is designed to align with the principles of the Kingdom’s Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL).

sections.b: Account and entity data, users’ work-related data, the Customer’s client data entered to conduct brokerage business (such as identity, contact details, and policy information), and usage and technical logs.
Operating and delivering the Platform, issuing policies and invoices, sending operational notifications, preventing fraud and meeting regulatory obligations, and improving performance and support.
Performance of the contract, compliance with regulatory obligations on brokerages, legitimate interest in securing and improving the service, and consent where required by law.

We may use trusted sub-processors (such as an email, SMS, or payment-gateway provider) only as needed to operate the service, under appropriate confidentiality and data-protection obligations. We do not sell personal data.
We retain data for the subscription term and as required by regulatory obligations (such as financial and regulatory record-keeping), after which it is deleted or anonymized per approved policies.

Subject to law, a data subject has the right to be informed, to access, rectify, delete, and object within legal limits. These rights are usually exercised through the brokerage (the controller), which we support as a processor.
We apply reasonable technical and organizational controls including encryption in transit, tenant data isolation, role-based access control, immutable audit logs, and periodic backups.
Platform data is hosted inside the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. It is not transferred outside the Kingdom except where legally justified and with the required safeguards.
For any privacy question or request, contact the NX Solutions team via the contact form on the home page.

dpa.title: Data Processing Agreement
dpa.intro: This agreement governs NX Solutions’ processing of personal data on the Customer’s behalf, and supplements the Terms of Service.

sections.b: The Customer acts as the “controller” of its client and employee data, and NX Solutions acts as the “processor” that processes data per the Customer’s documented instructions and in compliance with law.

Processing is limited to what is necessary to provide the Platform services (storage, processing, issuing documents and invoices, notifications). The processor does not process data for its own purposes.

The processor may engage sub-processors to operate the service, binds them to data-protection obligations no less than this agreement, remains responsible for their performance, and notifies the Customer of any material change.
The processor applies technical and organizational controls appropriate to the risk, including encryption in transit, access control, tenant isolation, audit logs, and backups.
The processor notifies the Customer without undue delay upon becoming aware of a personal-data breach, with the available information to help the Customer meet its regulatory obligations.
The processor provides reasonable assistance to the Customer in responding to data-subject requests and in impact assessments and consultations with competent authorities where applicable.
On termination of the service the processor deletes or returns Customer data at the Customer’s choice within a reasonable period, unless law requires retention.
The processor provides the information needed to demonstrate compliance and allows — on reasonable arrangement and under confidentiality — appropriate audits of data-protection controls.

sla.title: Service Level & Support
sla.intro: This document sets out the Platform’s availability targets and support response times by plan.

sections.b: We target 99.9% monthly Platform availability, excluding scheduled maintenance windows and circumstances beyond reasonable control. “Availability” means access to the Platform’s core functions.
Planned maintenance is generally performed outside peak hours with reasonable prior notice when material impact is expected, and is not counted as downtime.
First response time depends on the plan: up to 24 business hours for Basic, up to 4 hours for Professional, and up to 2 hours for Enterprise. These are first-response, not final-resolution, times.
Support is provided via the channels published inside the Platform and the official email, with priority support for higher plans.
Periodic data backups are taken within the in-Kingdom hosting environment, with recovery procedures aimed at minimizing data loss and downtime.
This document’s commitments exclude failures caused by Customer misuse, third parties beyond our control, force majeure, or scheduled maintenance.
Incidents are reported via the published support channels and classified by severity to prioritize handling and response.

documentsCenter.title: Documents
documentsCenter.subtitle: Central repository for all company documents — official & attachments, with filters and search

products.title: Products
products.subtitle: Insurance-line portfolio — production, commission, clients, and loss ratio per line
